name: MS Porta Cabin
description:
MS Porta Cabin — IS 2062 Grade A Mild Steel Frame, Factory-Built

Every structural member in a SAMAN MS porta cabin — base frame, top frame, wall supports, and roof members — is fabricated from IS 2062 Grade A mild steel, the same material standard required for structural steel in permanent construction in India.

IS 2062 Grade A — The Standard in Every SAMAN MS Frame

IS 2062 Grade A is not a marketing claim — it is a Bureau of Indian Standards certification for the material used in structural applications. For a construction site manager or EPC contractor procuring porta cabins for a formal project, this matters for one specific reason: it is the same standard specified in project procurement documents, government site contracts, and industrial zone supplier qualifications.

SAMAN sources IS 2062 Grade A certified mild steel for every MS porta cabin base frame and structural member. The certification is traceable — mill test certificates for the steel used in your order are available on request for procurement submission.

IS 2062 Grade A certified mild steel C-section — 100×50mm base frame member used in every SAMAN MS porta cabin. The same material standard required for structural steel in permanent buildings in India. Mill test certificates available on request for project procurement submissions.

Every SAMAN MS porta cabin is fabricated and quality-inspected at our Bangalore and Greater Noida facilities before dispatch. Call or WhatsApp +91 97089 89937 to confirm specifications, sizes, and project pricing within 24 hours.

MS or FRP — One Paragraph That Makes the Decision

The material choice between mild steel and fibre-reinforced plastic comes down to two questions: how long will the cabin be deployed, and how hard will it be used?

Choose MS for: deployments of 2 years or more, sites with high daily foot traffic (25+ users per day), coastal or high-humidity locations where you want a surface-treated steel warranty, and any application requiring floor loads above 500 kg — bunkhouses, site offices with heavy equipment, storage rooms with stacked material. An MS porta cabin with proper surface treatment and annual maintenance lasts 20 to 25 years.

Choose FRP for: short-duration deployments under 6 months, applications where lightweight movement between sites is a priority, and event or temporary facility use where the unit needs to be relocated frequently without crane access.

For buyers who want to compare the broader steel cabin category before committing to the MS specification, our steel porta cabin page covers the full steel overview.

MS mild steel porta cabin (right) and FRP cabin (left) after comparable site deployment — FRP shows corner stress cracks and UV surface yellowing. MS with intact surface treatment shows no structural degradation. For deployments of 2 years or more, the surface-treated MS cabin has lower total cost of ownership.

Surface Treatment — How SAMAN MS Cabins Hold Up Over 20+ Years

Rust is the most common objection raised against MS porta cabins. It is a valid concern — and it is entirely preventable when surface treatment is done correctly before the cabin leaves the factory.

Every SAMAN MS porta cabin goes through the following surface treatment process before dispatch: blast cleaning of all fabricated steel surfaces to Sa 2.5 standard, application of zinc phosphate primer in two coats, followed by a polyurethane topcoat in the specified colour. The total dry film thickness of the coating system is 100 to 125 microns minimum. This is applied in controlled factory conditions — not on site after erection.

Zinc phosphate primer application on MS porta cabin wall panels inside SAMAN's manufacturing facility — Sa 2.5 blast-cleaned surface, two-coat primer followed by PU topcoat in specified colour. Total dry film thickness 100–125 microns minimum. Applied in controlled factory conditions before dispatch, not on site.

The maintenance requirement to achieve the full 20 to 25 year structural lifespan is straightforward: repaint the external surfaces every 2 to 3 years using a compatible polyurethane topcoat, and inspect the base frame weld joints and skid feet annually for any surface break. No specialist contractor is required — your site maintenance team handles both.

FRP cabins require no painting but crack under mechanical impact and degrade in UV exposure over 10 to 15 years. For permanent site deployments, the surface-treated MS cabin has a lower total cost of ownership over the full project life.

Frame Specification — What the Numbers Mean for Your Site

The structural frame of a SAMAN MS porta cabin is built to carry real working loads — not just its own weight.

Base frame: 100×50mm C-section members fabricated from 9mm IS 2062 Grade A plate. This is the same section profile specified in government porta cabin technical documents for construction site bunkhouses. Floor live load capacity: 1 tonne across the full cabin floor area.

Top frame: 50×50mm MS square hollow section at all four upper corners and ridge. Carries roof dead load and wind load.

Wall panel supports: 40×40mm MS square pipe stiffeners at standard centres, supporting corrugated MS wall sheets in 16 to 18 gauge.

Roof: MS corrugated sheet with a 3-degree slope for water drainage, supported by purlin members at 600mm centres. Roof live load capacity: 500 kg — suitable for minor equipment storage on the roof if access is provided.

These specifications confirm that the SAMAN MS porta cabin can serve as a permanent site office, supervisor's cabin, security post, or worker bunkhouse for the full duration of a long-duration infrastructure project.

SAMAN MS porta cabin base frame visible during crane repositioning — 100×50mm IS 2062 Grade A C-section longitudinal members and cross-members at 1-metre intervals. Floor live load capacity 1 tonne. The same base frame specification used in government construction site bunkhouse procurement documents.

MS Porta Cabin Price — Starting Cost and What Moves It

A standard SAMAN MS porta cabin starts at Rs 1,050 per sq ft for a basic 10×10 ft single-room unit — door, sliding window, IS 2062 frame, surface treatment, and factory inspection included. Standard configurations range from Rs 1,050 to Rs 1,800 per sq ft depending on cabin size, insulation grade, window count, and internal fit-out.

What moves the cost within this range: cabin size (larger cabins have lower per-sq-ft cost), insulation specification (50mm PUF insulated panels cost more than non-insulated), number of windows and doors, electrical fit-out, and distance from our Bangalore or Greater Noida facilities.

For a multi-unit order of 5 or more cabins of the same configuration, SAMAN confirms a project-specific price at the quote stage.

A 6-unit SAMAN MS porta cabin compound at an Indian infrastructure project site — matching specification, colour, and documentation across the full order. Project pricing for 5 or more units is confirmed at the quote stage. Delivery from Bangalore and Greater Noida to sites across India in 7 to 21 working days.

For buyers with a tighter budget who need the MS frame standard but want the most economical specification, our low-cost porta cabin page covers entry-level MS configurations.

For the full SAMAN porta cabin range — sizes, rent vs buy, delivery timelines, and all material options — see our porta cabins hub page.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the difference between IS 2062 Grade A and Grade B steel in a porta cabin frame?

IS 2062 Grade A has a minimum yield strength of 250 MPa and is the standard grade for general structural applications including porta cabin frames. Grade B offers slightly higher yield strength (300 MPa) but is specified for heavier structural applications like crane girders — it is not required for a porta cabin frame and is not cost-effective for this application. All SAMAN MS porta cabins use Grade A, which meets every construction site procurement specification in India.

How long will an MS porta cabin last on a coastal or high-humidity site in India?

A SAMAN MS porta cabin correctly surface-treated and maintained will last 20 to 25 years even on coastal sites. The zinc phosphate primer creates a passivation layer that resists chloride ion attack from sea air. The critical maintenance step is repainting every 2 to 3 years before the topcoat degrades — once substrate steel is exposed to salt air, surface rusting accelerates. Cabins on coastal sites that are repainted on schedule consistently achieve the full lifespan.

Can an MS porta cabin be modified after delivery — additional doors cut, windows added, or internal walls installed?

Yes. MS porta cabins can be field-modified because the frame and panel system is welded steel — a fabricator can cut openings, weld in new door frames, add internal partition walls, or install mezzanine decking after delivery. FRP cabins cannot be field-cut without compromising the panel bond. Confirm any planned modifications at the order stage — SAMAN can factory-fit additional openings before dispatch at lower cost than field cutting.

Tell us your site location, cabin size, insulation requirement, and number of units.

We will confirm specification, price, and delivery timeline within 24 hours.

Call or WhatsApp: +91 97089 89937
